seoTitle: How to Pay With PayPal on Steam (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to buy games and DLC on Steam using PayPal, including how to save your payment details for faster future checkouts.
intro: Funding your Steam library shouldn't mean typing card numbers into every purchase screen. This guide walks through selecting PayPal at checkout and saving your account for one-click purchases going forward. It's for PC gamers who prefer to keep their card details tucked inside PayPal instead.

steps.heading: Add a game to your cart
steps.body: In the Steam client, find your game and click through to your shopping cart.

steps.heading: Choose Purchase for myself
steps.body: Click the green Purchase for myself button to begin checkout.

steps.heading: Select PayPal as your payment method
steps.body: Open the Payment Method dropdown and choose PayPal, then click Continue.

steps.heading: Log in through the secure overlay
steps.body: A PayPal browser window will open; enter your credentials, pass security checks, and choose your preferred funding asset.

steps.heading: Save your details and confirm
steps.body: Check the box to save your account for future logins, click Agree & Continue, then finish with the green Purchase button back on Steam.

faq.question: Can I use a debit card linked to PayPal instead of my balance?
faq.answer: Yes, once the PayPal overlay opens you can pick any linked funding source including checking accounts or debit cards.

faq.question: Does saving my PayPal info to Steam pose a security risk?
faq.answer: Saved details are handled by PayPal's authentication layer, so your card and bank numbers aren't stored directly by Steam.

faq.question: Can I remove PayPal from Steam later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can update or remove saved payment methods anytime from your Steam account settings.

faq.question: Will my purchase show up immediately in my library?
faq.answer: Yes, once the transaction completes and redirects back to Steam, your new game appears in your library ready to download.
